• ✅ Axis Bank has completed the pricing of a US$100 million tranche of USD-denominated Additional Tier 1 (AT1) Notes under its Global Medium Term Notes (GMTN) Programme. 

• 📊 The new tranche will be consolidated with the existing US$500 million 6.875% AT1 Notes issued on June 30, 2026, taking the total series size to US$600 million.

Tam for EMS industry 

India is emerging as a key global electronics manufacturing hub, with the EMS industry expected to grow at 25-30% CAGR over FY24-29E. 

Growth is driven by the China+1 shift, electronics exports reaching ~USD48b in FY26 (+25% YoY), and policy support through PLI, INR400b ECMS and semiconductor initiatives. 

With India accounting for only 4-5% of the global EMS market, increasing component localization and export-led manufacturing provide a long runway for growth.

EMS companies which will go backwards into component manufacturing are the ones where complete integrated business models will be created

GENERAL INSURANCE INDUSTRY | June 2026 Premium Update

Key Highlights
- General insurance: +14.3% YoY
- SAHI segment: +31% YoY
- Niva Bupa outperforms
- ICICI Lombard in line
- Go Digit reports decline

Industry Overview
- General insurance industry premium grew 14.3% YoY in June 2026.
- Standalone Health Insurers (SAHI) recorded a stronger 31% YoY premium growth.
- Non-life insurance industry premium reached ₹87,825 crore during Apr–Jun FY27, up 10.7% YoY.

Company Performance
- ICICI Lombard: Premium grew 13.7–14% YoY to ₹2,259 crore, broadly in line with industry.
- New India Assurance: Premium increased 10% YoY.
- Bajaj Allianz General Insurance: Premium rose 21.8% YoY to ₹1,760 crore.
- Go Digit: Premium declined 2.8–3% YoY to ₹720 crore.

Health Insurance
- Niva Bupa: Premium jumped 34.3–35% YoY to ₹798 crore, outperforming the SAHI industry.
- Star Health: Premium increased 19.2% YoY to ₹1,594 crore, below the SAHI industry's growth rate.

Key Takeaway
- Premium growth remained healthy across the insurance sector, with health insurers continuing to outperform. Niva Bupa led the growth charts, while Go Digit remained the only major listed player to report a decline.

- LME brand registration received

Business Updates

LME Approval
- Kutch Copper Ltd. (KCL), a wholly owned subsidiary of Adani Enterprises, has received London Metal Exchange (LME) brand registration.
- The approval covers 'Adani Copper' Grade-A copper cathodes.
- LME warrants will be available from July 10, 2026.

- LME registration enhances global market access and international credibility.
- It also improves financing flexibility and strengthens India's position in the global copper supply chain.

Key Takeaway
- LME registration is a significant milestone that enhances Kutch Copper's global acceptance and supports Adani's long-term growth in the copper business.

EMKAY ON TYRE STOCKS 

We upgrade Apollo/CEAT to BUY from Add/Reduce and reiterate BUY on JK Tyre, owing to increased confidence in recovery of industry earnings amid a stronger demand/pricing environment.

Strong demand / Pricing enviornment to drive H2 recovery*

Over the last few months, tyre stocks have corrected sharply (~12-32%) from recent peaks on concerns around demand slowdown and sharp margin pressure from elevated crude and rubber prices. 

While near-term margins (H1 subdued, with Q1, the bottom quarter) will remain under stress, we believe the market is undermining the sharp recovery in the industry's earnings cycle. 

We remain constructive on the tyres space, supported by a confluence of factors: sustained strong replacement demand triggered by GST cuts; strong recovery in OEM demand; pricing discipline with ~8–9% cumulative price hikes taken by the industry over the past 3–4M aided by tight demand-supply dynamics; peaking of the RM basket with crude reverting to earlier levels; and sharp valuation correction. 

We raise FY28E EPS by ~17-28% and lift our TPs to Rs550 (from Rs425) for *Apollo Tyres* (16x FY28E PER), Rs4,500 (vs Rs3,600) for *CEAT* (18x FY28E PER vs 20x earlier), and Rs650 (from Rs550) for *JK Tyre* (14x FY28E PER), driven by strong demand and improved pricing outlook.

**TRENT | Citi View**

**Key Highlights**
- Sell rating maintained
- Target Price at ₹2,733
- Revenue missed estimates
- Revenue productivity remained weak
- Store additions ahead of estimates
- Competitive pressures persist

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **Citi** maintained its **Sell** rating with a **Target Price of ₹2,733**.
- **Revenue:** **Q1 FY27 standalone revenue** grew **19% YoY**, below Citi's **23% estimate**.
- **Revenue Productivity:** **Average revenue per square foot** (assuming similar new-store size as TTM) **declined 12.2% YoY**, indicating continued weakness despite a favourable base.
- **Store Expansion:** **Westside** added **1 net store** and **Zudio** added **19 net stores** during the quarter, ahead of Citi's expectations of **0** and **6** stores, respectively.
- **Seasonality:** Citi noted that **Q1 is typically the weakest quarter for store additions**, making the expansion pace less of a concern.
- **Outlook:** The brokerage remains **cautious** due to **weak revenue per square foot, rising competition, store cannibalization and continued expansion into Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities**.

**Key Takeaway**
- Citi maintains a **negative stance on Trent**, believing that **declining store productivity, intensifying competition and expansion-related challenges** could weigh on future growth despite continued store additions.

**Brokerage Updates**

- **Store Expansion:** **Westside** added **1 net store**, while **Zudio** added **19 net stores** during the quarter.

**Key Takeaway**
- Bernstein continues to **favour Trent**, believing the **slight revenue miss and slower store additions are temporary**, while maintaining confidence in the company's **FY27 expansion plans and long-term growth trajectory**.

**TRENT | Morgan Stanley View**

**Key Highlights**
- Overweight rating maintained
- Target Price at ₹3,151
- Revenue broadly in line
- Store additions moderated
- Margin improvement expected
- Near-term stock weakness possible

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **Morgan Stanley (MS)** maintained its **Overweight (OW)** rating with a **Target Price of ₹3,151**.
- **Revenue:** **Q1 FY27 standalone revenue (excluding GST)** grew **19% YoY**, slightly below Morgan Stanley's **21% estimate**, and broadly in line with **20% growth in Q4 FY26**.
- **Store Expansion:** The company added **19 net Zudio stores** and **1 Westside store** during the quarter, reflecting a **slower expansion pace** compared to the strong additions in Q4 FY26.
- **Margins:** Morgan Stanley expects **Q1 FY27 standalone EBITDA margin** to improve **100 bps YoY** to **18.5%**, compared with **18.6% in Q4 FY26**.
- **Stock View:** With the stock having **rallied around 20% over the past month**, Morgan Stanley believes the **slightly weaker-than-expected revenue print could lead to near-term profit booking**.

**Key Takeaway**
- Morgan Stanley remains **constructive on Trent's long-term outlook**, supported by **healthy revenue growth and margin expansion**, but expects **near-term stock performance to remain subdued** following the recent rally and slightly softer Q1 business update.

**JUBILANT FOODWORKS | Morgan Stanley View**

**Key Highlights**
- Equal-Weight rating maintained
- Target Price at ₹486
- India business met expectations
- LFL growth improved sequentially
- Store expansion continued
- Margins remain key monitorable

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **Morgan Stanley (MS)** maintained its **Equal-Weight (EW)** rating with a **Target Price of ₹486**.
- **India Business:** Standalone **Q1 FY27 revenue** grew **9.2% YoY**, broadly in line with Morgan Stanley's **9% estimate**.
- **Same-Store Sales:** **Like-for-Like (LFL) growth** improved to **2.5%**, compared with **0.2% in Q4 FY26**, but was slightly below the brokerage's **3% estimate**.
- **Store Expansion:** The company added **58 new stores** during the quarter, ahead of Morgan Stanley's expectation of **50 stores**, resulting in a **12% YoY increase in store count**.
- **Consolidated Revenue:** Increased **14.1% YoY**, below Morgan Stanley's **18% growth estimate**.
- **Outlook:** Morgan Stanley noted that the **sequential improvement in India growth** is in line with management guidance, but **LFL growth remains below the company's long-term target of 5–7%**.
- **Margins:** The brokerage expects **standalone EBITDA margin** to moderate to **20%** in Q1 FY27 from **20.5% in Q4 FY26**, citing **inflationary pressures** as the key monitorable.

**Key Takeaway**
- Morgan Stanley remains **neutral on Jubilant FoodWorks**, acknowledging the **improving sales trajectory and healthy store expansion**, while highlighting that **margin pressure and the pace of same-store sales recovery** will be the key factors to watch in the upcoming results.

__Disclaimer: This post is only for educational and learning purposes. No Buy, Sell or Hold recommendation.__

**TITAN COMPANY | Citi View**

**Key Highlights**
- Buy rating maintained
- Target Price at ₹5,075
- Jewellery growth beat estimates
- Healthy festive demand
- Strong retail expansion
- Broad-based business growth

**Brokerage Updates**
- **Rating:** **Citi** maintained its **Buy** rating with a **Target Price of ₹5,075**.
- **Jewellery Performance:** **Domestic jewellery (excluding bullion)** reported **39% YoY growth**, ahead of **Citi's estimate of 35%**, despite **gold prices rising around 59% YoY**.
- **Growth Drivers:** Management attributed the strong performance to **healthy festive demand**, including **Akshaya Tritiya**, during the quarter.
- **Consumer Trends:** **Buyer growth** remained in the **early double digits**, while the **average ticket size** increased by **high double digits**.
- **Product Mix:** Both **plain gold** and **studded jewellery** businesses grew in the **mid-30% range**, indicating a **stable studded jewellery mix** (excluding gold coins).
- **Retail Expansion:** **Tanishq, Mia, Zoya and beYon** added **22 new stores** across India during the quarter.
- **Other Businesses:** **CaratLane** delivered **42% YoY growth**, while **Watches & Wearables (+23%)**, **EyeCare (+23%)** and **Emerging Businesses (+19%)** also reported strong growth.

**Key Takeaway**
- Citi remains **positive on Titan**, supported by **better-than-expected jewellery growth, healthy consumer demand, continued retail expansion and strong performance across all major business segments**, reinforcing confidence in its long-term growth outlook.

FPI FLOW | Financial Sector Sees Strongest Buying in 2026

Key Highlights
- ₹14,634 Cr buying in financials
- Highest fortnightly inflow in 2026
- FPIs turned net buyers
- Buying after 3 months
- Financials led the inflows
- Positive for market sentiment

Business Updates
- Financial Sector: Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) bought shares worth ₹14,634 Cr in the second half of June 2026.
- Record Buying: This marks the highest fortnightly purchase in the financial sector by FPIs so far in 2026.
- Trend Reversal: FPIs turned net buyers after three consecutive months of net outflows.
- Sectoral Flows: Apart from financial services, FPIs also witnessed strong buying in construction, consumer services and other sectors.
- Market Impact: Sustained foreign inflows into heavyweight financial stocks are expected to support overall market sentiment and liquidity.

Key Takeaway
- The sharp return of FPI buying, particularly in financials, signals improving foreign investor confidence in Indian equities, which could provide near-term support to the broader market if the trend continues.

Emerging Retail Trend
- Small-format grocery chains are expanding by focusing on value-driven, planned shopping rather than instant delivery.

Business Model

Why Consumers Prefer the Format
- Transparent pricing.
- Wider product assortment.
- Better merchandising and shopping experience.
- Lower overall grocery bills.
- Convenient neighbourhood locations.

Value vs Quick Commerce
- Quick Commerce: Competes on speed and convenience for urgent purchases.
- Value Supermarkets: Compete on affordability, offering 5–10% savings on larger grocery baskets.
- Consumers continue to prefer kiranas for emergency purchases while using value supermarkets for planned shopping.

Expansion Trends
- SuperK: 130+ stores across Andhra Pradesh, Telangana and Karnataka.
- Apna Mart: 200+ stores across Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh, West Bengal and Bihar.

Key Takeaway
- India's grocery retail market is witnessing the emergence of a third retail format between kirana stores and quick commerce. THE DRAGON IN THE SUPPLY CHAIN – I | Electronics: Assembly Boom, Component Gap

Key Highlights
- Electronics exports hit record highs
- Smartphones dominate exports
- Component imports remain high
- Ecosystem depth is the next challenge

India's Electronics Growth
- Electronics exports reached a record ~US$48 billion in FY26.
- Smartphone exports exceeded US$31 billion, driven by global manufacturing expansion and exports to the US and Europe.
- India has become the world's second-largest mobile phone manufacturer, with production rising nearly 28x since FY15.

The Structural Challenge
- Domestic value addition in smartphones is estimated at only 18–20%.
- Integrated manufacturing ecosystems, such as China's, achieve 35–45% value addition.
- India continues to rely heavily on imported:
  - Semiconductor chips
  - Display assemblies
  - Camera modules
  - PCB components
  - Sensors and connectors
  - Manufacturing equipment
  - Specialty industrial materials

Global Perspective
- Global electronics manufacturing operates through interconnected supply chains.
- Even China imports advanced semiconductor chips from Taiwan and South Korea, while sourcing specialised equipment and materials from Japan.
- Vietnam also depends significantly on imported electronic components.
- The key differentiator is the depth of domestic manufacturing capabilities.

What India Needs
- Semiconductor fabrication and packaging
- Display manufacturing
- Electronic component ecosystem
- Advanced materials and manufacturing equipment
- Higher domestic value addition across the supply chain

Key Takeaway
- India's electronics industry has successfully emerged as a global assembly and export hub, but the next phase of growth depends on developing a deeper domestic component ecosystem. Building capabilities in semiconductors, displays, electronics components and advanced manufacturing will be essential for increasing value addition, reducing import dependence and strengthening long-term competitiveness.

**CLSA on Industrials**

Indian government has granted a two-year exemption to four Chinese electrical equipment manufacturers with manufacturing units in India, allowing them to participate in government/CPSE tenders.

More important is that this exemption was granted at request of Ministry of Power, validating case led by complaints from transmission utilities struggling with price inflation on controlled supply chains

Do not see this move impacting near-term volume growth, but increased competition (ex-HVDC converters) is likely to weigh on pricing power & margins of domestic T&D equipment cos such as Hitachi, GE Vernova T&D, BHEL (U-PF) & CG Power

Investors should be wary of lofty valuations (PE of 50-90x) on historic peak high margins led by ‘Make in India” driven shield but now govt seems to have taken cognisance of excessive margins being made by transformer sector.

**Stock Stats | Stock info | Infographics
****@Stockinfo333**

**Macquarie on Industrials**

As per industry sources, government is set to exempt 4 Chinese players (detailed below), having manufacturing units in India, from the restriction imposed in February 2023 for critical power projects.

This exemption is valid for a period of two years (till June 2028)

Most of grid companies are in midst of capacity expansion and are likely to add capacity over the next 2-3 year, which is the validity of this exemption.

Should this exemption be to extend in future, long-term negative impact on margins of the incumbents, especially the 765kV GIS players cannot be ruled out

Barring TBEA, most of other companies have sub-scale operations in India & are likely to take time to ramp up

Believe this exemption is to address current shortage of certain of EHV grid equipment & unlikely to allow large scale Chinese imports in sensitive areas of grid infrastructure.

Media reports highlight that government has approved four Chinese companies (TBEA Energy, Nanjing Electric, New Northeast Electric, Taikai Electric) to supply equipment for government projects only from their Indian manufacturing facilities.

Import restrictions still remain.

Even with supply from these facilities, believe demand-supply mismatch remains.

DAM Capital write up:

.HDFCBank - 1Q27 Biz Update: Better but not the best as one wanted. 

• Gross loans stood at Rs30.6 trn, up 15% YoY and 3% QoQ – highest YoY growth since merger. AUM was Rs31.3 trn, up 12% YoY and 2% QoQ.

• Deposits came in at Rs31.7 trn, up 15% YoY and 2% QoQ. **On average basis seasonal sequential pace is maintained but slower YoY, barring CASA which has base effect.**

• CASA stood at Rs10.3 trn, up 9% YoY but down 3% QoQ (from CA decline generally). **CASA ratio was at 32.3% **- This is now almost like south based regional pvt banks and with FCNR this could look more lower.

• Term deposits grew strong by 17% YoY and 5% QoQ.

• C-D ratio (on gross loans) was 96.5%, compared to 95.3% in 4Q26 & 96.0% in 1Q26.

Systematix write up:

HDFC Bank 1QFY27 business figures – as expected the CD ratio has deteriorated* 

*Advances*

Average advances under Management have grown 2.5% QoQ and 10.8% YoY to Rs. 30,386 bn

Period end advances under Management have grown 2.3% QoQ and 12.4% YoY to Rs. 31,270 bn

Gross advances have grown 3.4% QoQ and 15.4% YoY to Rs 30,610 bn

*Deposits*

Average deposits have grown 5.6% QoQ and 13.3% YoY to Rs. 30,114 bn

Average CASA deposits have grown 4.2% QoQ and 11.2% YoY to Rs. 9,570 bn

Average Time deposits have grown 6.3% QoQ and 14.3% YoY to Rs. 20,544 bn

Total deposits at period end have grown 2.1% QoQ and 14.7% YoY to Rs. 31,705 bn

CASA deposits at period end have de-grown -3.3% QoQ but have grown 9.4% YoY to Rs. 10,255 bn

Time deposits at period end have grown 4.9% QoQ and 17.4% YoY to Rs. 21,450 bn

The period-end CASA ratio stood at 32.3%, down by -180bps QoQ and -155bps YoY

The calculated Credit to Deposit ratio has deteriorated to 95.8%, up 122bps QoQ and 67bps YoY

*Valuation and Recommendation* 

HDFC Bank (HDFCB) trades at an FY27E P/ABV of 1.7x for an FY27E/FY28E RoE profile of 14.0%/14.3%. We currently have a BUY rating on the stock.

Jefferies on India Industrials

Media reports highlight that the government has approved four Chinese companies to supply equipment for government projects only from their Indian manufacturing facilities

Import restrictions still remain

Even with supply from these facilities, believe demand-supply mismatch remains

Remain positive on Hitachi Energy and Siemens Energy and see the correction as a buying opportunity

Remain constructive on Power T&D with supply to lag demand

Hitachi Energy and Siemens Energy are top picks given their strong 40%+ earnings CAGR on operating leverage backed by strong revenue visibility

Macquarie on India Industrials

Allowing Chinese entities in government contracts

Move is further to government intention to ease shortfall of critical components

Most of the grid companies are in the midst of capacity expansion

Likely to add capacity over the next 2-3 year, which is the validity of this exemption

Should this exemption be to extend in future, long term negative impact on margins of the incumbents

Negative impact on margins especially for the 765kV GIS players cannot be ruled out

Barring TBEA, most of the other companies have sub-scale operations in India and are likely to take time to ramp up

Believe this exemption is to address the current shortage of certain of EHV grid equipment

Unlikely to allow large scale Chinese imports in the sensitive areas of grid infrastructure

👟 Liberty Accelerates Growth With 100 New Stores

Liberty is entering a new growth phase with plans to open 100 stores, including exclusive Healers outlets, while investing ₹75 crore in retail expansion, brand building, and customer experience.

📌 Key Highlights

🏬 Opening 100 new stores, expanding the retail network beyond 600 outlets.

💰 Investing ₹75 crore: ₹50 crore for retail expansion and ₹25 crore for marketing & brand building.

🎯 Streamlined the portfolio from 10 sub-brands to 6 focused brands.

👟 Healers contributes nearly 40% of revenue, while Leap 7X contributes around 20%. Both are expected to become standalone brands in the next 3–4 years.

🛍️ Exclusive Healers stores to open across airports, malls, and pop-up formats.

✨ Rolling out modern store formats to enhance customer experience.

🤖 Investing in AI-driven engagement.

🏭 Manufacturing investments focused on automation and efficiency, not capacity expansion.

🌍 Exports contribute less than 5% of revenue, with significant long-term potential.

Global Semiconductor Supply Chain Remains Highly Concentrated

Key Highlights:

🏭 TSMC controls over 80% of the world's leading-edge (<16nm) chip production, powering most AI servers and premium smartphones.

🌏 Taiwan, South Korea, and China account for ~85% of global semiconductor foundry revenue, highlighting regional concentration.

💾 Samsung and SK Hynix produce ~70% of the world's DRAM memory chips.

⚙️ ASML holds a near-100% monopoly in EUV lithography, the critical technology required to manufacture chips below 7nm.

🇺🇸 US companies generate around 60% of global chip design revenue, while China accounts for ~38% of global OSAT (chip packaging and testing) capacity.

🏗️ 56% of global wafer capacity still lies in mature nodes (≥34nm), creating an opportunity for emerging semiconductor manufacturing hubs like India.
